message: fix follow-up questions semantics and add a refine section. Backend, types, and UI are done;
post:
When building AURA (auratriage.vercel.app), we needed to refine how the AI asks follow-up questions. Initially, the AI asked questions that were meant for the user to ask the AI.

This led to confusion. The goal is to make the AI a better conversational partner, capable of asking clarifying questions to improve its own assessment, and also providing questions the user might want to ask.

We've now separated these into two distinct types: `clarifyingQuestions` (for the AI to ask the user) and `suggestedFollowUpQuestions` (for the user to ask the AI). This change makes the interaction more intuitive and the AI more helpful.

How do you approach differentiating AI-driven vs. user-driven questions in conversational interfaces?
alt: Code block showing the Gemini API route defining clarifyingQuestions and suggestedFollowUpQuestions for the AI's response.
